currentFeel:
Switchboard is a product with a genuinely top-decile design CEILING and an anxiety-inducing FLOOR, and the gap between them — not any single bug — is the core problem. At its best (Home's signed "weeknote" letter from Alex, the Results "— Riley" pullquote, the risk-gated swipe with rubber-band physics, the warm/honest empty states, the v6 marketing landing, the read-only Alex/Riley AgentPanel) it feels like a calm, premium, trustworthy colleague you'd hand your ad budget to. That warm-editorial restraint — warm-white canvas, one operator-amber action color, agent hues for identity only, editorial serif + tabular numerics — is exactly the right aesthetic bet for a time-poor, non-technical, mobile-first operator trusting AI to spend their money and talk to their customers, and it is real, intentional, and largely consistent in light mode.

But the everyday surface is calmer than it is ALIVE, and the decisive moments feel UNFINISHED. The product loses trust in exactly the places that carry the most of it. Three patterns recur across all 15 audits: (1) The trust-critical instant — committing a real money-spending or customer-facing action — behaves like a prototype: the approval detail's "What this changes" panel ships literal em-dashes labelled "preview not yet wired / wiring it up next week" with "Money at risk: —", the Undo safety net is an un-themed shadcn box that never auto-dismisses and silently evicts on the next approval, approving on the main Inbox page never refreshes the feed or the "things need you" count (stale up to 60s), and there is no optimistic/in-flight feedback so the card freezes on faith. (2) The product's own SOUL is offstage: the lovingly hand-built breathing OperatorCharacter and 1,100+ lines of animated agent sprites are disconnected from every daily screen — Home shows agents as flat letter-discs, the AgentPanel hardcodes the avatar to state="idle", and the agents literally never move; the same agent also wears 2-5 different faces/colors one tap apart. (3) The foundation is FOUR design systems wearing a trench coat (globals HSL vars, the hardcoded-hex 'T' cockpit, Mercury, v6 landing) that quietly disagree on the brand's own colors and ship a Light/Dark/System toggle that breaks ~70% of surfaces and can auto-trigger on a dark-OS phone with zero user action. The net: a $100/mo premium frame undercut, at the moments that matter most, by a hobbyist finish.
designSystemState:
The design system is INCOHERENT AT THE FOUNDATION despite a tasteful, well-intentioned surface — this is the single most-cited structural problem, flagged independently by the design-system, typography, color/a11y, emotional-design, agent-cockpit, and landing audits. Concretely:

FOUR PARALLEL TOKEN SYSTEMS that disagree: (1) globals.css HSL custom-properties consumed as hsl(var(--x)) — the canonical app system; (2) a hardcoded-hex 'T' object in cockpit/tokens.ts (18 literal hex/rgba values, ZERO var() references, rendered through ~60 inline style={{...T.x}} bindings) that is structurally un-themeable and un-rebrandable and CANNOT join the cascade; (3) the legacy Mercury register; (4) the v6 landing palette (duplicated in BOTH tailwind.config.ts AND landing-v6.css). VERIFIED: tokens.ts is all literal hex.

BRAND COLORS DRIFT 2-4x: each agent identity hue is defined multiple times with conflicting values. VERIFIED: Alex coral is hsl(14 70% 58%) in globals vs hsl(14 75% 55%) as v6.coral in tailwind vs #E07A53 in alex-config; Mira is hsl(270 45% 58%) / --agent-mira-deep 256 31% 45% in globals vs hardcoded #4A3A66 in inbox-agent-avatar.tsx. The agent-amber alone has ≥3 values (#c97b1a / #B8782E / #A07850). The marketing coral (14 75% 55%) is not even the in-app operator-amber (30 55% 46%), so the accent that sells a buyer changes the moment they sign in.

DARK MODE IS A SHIPPED LIABILITY: VERIFIED — use-theme.ts toggles .dark and in "system" mode auto-applies via prefers-color-scheme (so dark can activate on first load, no user action), exposed as a real 3-way toggle in Settings → Account, but only ~40 of ~145 :root tokens have a .dark value. Every editorial token (--ink/--cream/--hair/--serif), every agent identity token, every shadow, all of Mercury, and the entire hex 'T' cockpit have NO dark value — so Home, the Mira desk, and every cockpit render black-ink-on-dark, illegible.

NO ENFORCED SCALES: spacing tokens (--space-*) are dead code with zero consumers; real spacing is free-hand px (7/9/11/14/18/22 mixed with 8/16/24 — neither 4pt nor 8pt grid). Elevation is ~35 ad-hoc shadows with FOUR different shadow base colors, no z-index→elevation ladder, so the product never settles into one light source. Typography has ~6 font roles but Tailwind exposes only 3, no type-size scale (raw px everywhere), ~9 unsystematized letter-spacing values for the small-caps system, a dead "Instrument Sans" --font-display fallback that never loads, and data-tabular is a no-op so KPI numbers jitter in proportional Inter. globals.css also self-documents an --accent vs --editorial-accent naming collision (raw-triple vs complete-value) that produces invalid/transparent colors.

The encouraging news: the migration path is half-proven (mira-config.ts already expresses the cockpit accent as hsl(var(--agent-mira)) rather than hex), the identity-only-color discipline is explicitly encoded and followed, and a tokens.test.ts already exists to host a drift-guard. Unifying onto ONE token system is the keystone that unblocks dark mode, kills color drift, and makes every other craft fix (elevation, spacing, type) enforceable.

topGaps.gap: The trust-critical commit moment behaves like a prototype: approval detail ships a hardcoded em-dash 'What this changes' / 'Money at risk: —' grid literally labelled 'preview not yet wired / wiring it up next week', the Undo safety net is an un-themed shadcn toast that never auto-dismisses (TOAST_REMOVE_DELAY ~16min) and evicts on the next approval (TOAST_LIMIT=1), there is no optimistic/in-flight feedback so the card freezes for the network round-trip, and the already-built 'alreadyHandled' stale-409 path is never wired (Approve on a teammate-handled item silently closes with no confirmation).

topGaps.gap: Approving on the main Inbox page never refreshes the feed or the 'things need you' count — useRecommendationAction invalidates ONLY recommendations.all() and audit.all(), never decisions.feed(); no onMutate optimistic removal exists. Operator clears 3 items, header still says 5, items silently reappear/reorder on the 60s poll. The OLD header-drawer path DOES invalidate decisions.feed(), so the PRIMARY surface has the worse behavior.

topGaps.gap: FOUR parallel design-token systems disagree on the brand's own colors: the hardcoded-hex 'T' cockpit (zero var(), un-themeable) collides with globals HSL vars, and each agent identity hue is defined 2-4x with conflicting values (Alex coral 14 70% 58% vs 14 75% 55% vs #E07A53; Mira violet vs #4A3A66). Any color/a11y/dark fix must be made 3+ times or it regresses.

topGaps.gap: The product's soul is offstage: the breathing OperatorCharacter + 1,100+ lines of animated agent sprites are disconnected from every daily surface — Home renders flat letter-discs, the AgentPanel hardcodes the avatar to state='idle' so agents NEVER move, and the same agent wears 2-5 different faces/colors one tap apart (serif-letter-circle on Home vs hex pixel-sprite-square in panel). The strongest 'is this alive and on my side' signal was built and unplugged.
topGaps.surfaces: Home (daily anchor), agent cockpits/panels, Inbox rows — the central agent metaphor

topGaps.gap: Perceived-performance investment is backwards: only the marketing page has a polished skeleton; the three daily authed surfaces (Home, Inbox, Results) have NO loading.tsx and the Inbox in-component loading state is a bare unstyled 'Loading…' div with no CSS rule. Worse, the Inbox loading gate is the broken `if (filtered.isLoading)` (skipped during keys-pending) so it can flash 'That's everything' false-inbox-zero with pending approvals on screen — the exact bug Mira-desk already fixed with `!data && !error`.

topGaps.gap: A globally-mounted blue 'Operator Chat — Type a command' command-line widget floats on EVERY authed screen, in raw bg-blue-600 (a color nowhere in the warm system) with developer-console jargon — reframing the whole 'trustworthy colleague' product as a 'developer console you must learn commands for' and contradicting the Alex/Riley/Mira personas.

topGaps.gap: Home — the daily driver — cold-starts into the FALLBACK verdict ('we don't have a read on today') + empty-state copy that looks broken/asleep (no isLoading branch, no skeleton), ships a permanently-empty labelled 'work in progress' module that always reads 'No active handoffs', and has NO cumulative 'since you hired' trust anchor (its longest lens is 7 days) — so on a calm day the calm tips into thin/anxious and there is no accumulated-value reason to be glad you opened it.
topGaps.evidence: home-page.tsx no isLoading branch; workInProgressItems hardcoded []; grep 'since you hired' in components/home → none (it lives only in the agent-panel sheet)

topGaps.gap: WCAG floor is soft on the most trust-critical controls: the ONE amber 'approve/spend' primary button fails AA contrast at REST (white-on-amber 3.76:1 at 14px/600); the entire Inbox decision card is a pointer-only div with no tabIndex/role/onKeyDown (hard 2.1.1 keyboard failure on the core flow, no desktop two-pane keyboard model); there is no global :focus-visible rule (only ~4 elements have any visible focus); and failing micro-text tiers (tertiary 2.90:1, ink-4 ~2.4:1) carry real Results metric labels.

topGaps.gap: Motion is built but unwired exactly where operators feel it: framer-motion@12 is a paid dependency with ZERO usages in src, so list enter/exit/FLIP is absent — the Inbox card's data-exiting hook is dead, EXIT_MS=280 races the 320ms CSS transition, the queue snaps closed, and under reduced-motion a committed card teleports 600px (inline transform the global reset can't neutralize). Home has zero entrance choreography despite useEntrancePlayed + a fade-in keyframe existing unused.
topGaps.surfaces: Inbox card removal, Home arrival, route transitions (the 'alive desk' promise)

topGaps.gap: Onboarding disowns the app it onboards into and lets an untested money agent go live: the wizard uses near-black system-font CTAs (not amber) and a disabled-gray agent mark (not the identity hue / breathing aura), 'Go live anyway' launches a customer-messaging, money-spending Alex with ZERO passing tests one soft click away, and the launch payoff dumps the operator onto an empty 'All caught up · 0 open enquiries' Home with no 'you just launched' acknowledgment — while the built-for-this useFirstRun checklist sits as dead code.
topGaps.surfaces: Onboarding/activation (first-run to value) + the post-launch Home landing

topGaps.gap: The marketing funnel has no floor and small finish flaws undercut a premium frame: every primary CTA dead-ends in a mailto: email (a sold mobile buyer must compose an email; the real (auth)/onboarding + register API exist but are never linked), there is a doubled-word typo ('One you you don't recognize') on the page selling AI-written copy, and there is ZERO social proof — every number is stamped 'illustrative' so nothing on the page is verifiably real.
topGaps.surfaces: Marketing landing / first impression (top of funnel) — Riley/Mira beats are otherwise the best proof in the product
topGaps.evidence: pricing.tsx mailto: ctaHref; (auth)/onboarding + api/auth/register exist unused; beat-mira.tsx:129/234 'you you'; no testimonial/logo/founder component in components/landing

keepThese: The warm-editorial aesthetic bet itself — warm-white canvas, ONE operator-amber action color, agent hues for IDENTITY ONLY, editorial serif + tabular numerics. This discipline is explicitly encoded AND followed and is exactly right for a time-poor operator trusting AI with money; it is the product's biggest asset, not a thing to redesign.

The Home 'weeknote' — Alex's signed, first-person, drop-capped 'week-so-far note' on a Newsreader optical-size axis composed only from real numbers. Best-in-class editorial type craft; the single most love-worthy moment in the app. The model (a colleague's letter, not a dashboard) should be extended, never flattened.

The risk-gated swipe safety model: canSwipeApprove() only permits low-risk, no-side-effect items; anything financial/client-facing/external rubber-bands back and routes to detail, enforced at the predicate not inferred from copy. 'Reversible by default, friction proportional to stakes' is the correct trust posture.
The hand-tuned swipe physics (6px axis-lock deadzone, 0.65-power rubber-band, undamped intent tracking, trailing-click suppression) — real craft that separates a true swipe from a toy one.

The honest, warm, never-blaming, never-fabricating voice in the strong surfaces: failure microcopy that distinguishes 'saved but undelivered' from 'nothing was saved', empty/error states that NEVER collapse 'error' into 'all clear', plain-English risk chips instead of traffic lights, and a work-log that refuses to invent a 'since you last looked' time anchor. This honesty IS the product's emotional moat.

The read-only Alex/Riley AgentPanel 'three-states-never-collapse' invariant: each slot owns its own loading/error/empty branch, error never reads as '0', zero is shown dimmed-but-present. This is the gold standard the rest of the app should be measured against — promote it to a shared primitive.

The cumulative 'since you hired Alex' hero in the agent panel (56px serif numeral, eyebrow bound strictly to whichever window actually returned so the number is never mislabeled). Reframes the agent as an investment, not a feature — bring this framing to Home.

Mira's draft-only safety contract repeated everywhere it matters ('Nothing goes live without you'), cost-confirm gating with a live $ estimate, and the off-scope-redirect brief box that kills the blank-box freeze. The right friction in the right places for a surface that spends real provider money.

The v6 landing's Riley beat (live faux Meta-Ads dashboard that FLAGS the saturated ad set + a plain-English Approve/Edit/Dismiss draft card) and Mira pipeline — they show the governed, you-approve product working rather than listing features. Best sections in the product.

The honest-marketing guard tests (vitest suite banning ~40 over-claims: fake SLAs, non-existent integrations, fictional agents, 'free trial' framing). Codifying 'we will not lie on the landing page' is rare and exactly right for this money-on-the-line audience — keep and extend the pattern to in-app copy.

Globally-respected reduced-motion handling (two prefers-reduced-motion guards + token-driven transitions) and the performance-conscious OperatorCharacter (gradient aura not blur, pauses on hidden tab) — real accessibility/polish signals most teams skip.

The restrained 3-noun primary IA courage (Home/Inbox/Results) and the deliberate hiding (not deleting) of forensic surfaces like /activity — the right altitude for a non-technical operator; the fix is making it COHERENT across breakpoints, not adding more.
researchAgenda: TARGET FEEL / BENCHMARK: What is the precise target 'feel' in concrete, testable terms, and which 2-3 exemplars define each axis? The audits invoke Linear, Superhuman, Stripe, Mercury, Things as the bar — capture specifically what each does at the moments we're weak (Superhuman/Linear: optimistic list removal + FLIP + keyboard triage; Stripe/Mercury: skeleton discipline + money-confirm panels; Things: swipe physics). Define a small set of measurable feel-targets (e.g. 'card leaves <16ms after commit', 'every authed route paints a skeleton within one frame', 'AA-passing primary action') so 'amazing to use' becomes a checklist, not a vibe.

TOKEN UNIFICATION SAFETY: What is the safe, staged path to collapse the four token systems onto ONE (globals HSL vars), and what breaks? Specifically: can we mechanically rewrite cockpit tokens.ts 'T' + the *_ACCENT hex constants to hsl(var(--...)) the way mira-config.ts already does without visual regression — and which cockpit components are actually LIVE vs dead (ApprovalCard/roi-bar/kpi-strip appear orphaned)? Determine the minimum live footprint so the migration is scoped to what operators can actually see, and what drift-guard tests (extending the existing tokens.test.ts) prevent regression.

DARK MODE DECISION: Do our medspa-operator users actually want/need dark mode at launch, or should we ship-light-only until a complete .dark palette exists? Research the audience (mobile-first, likely default-dark phones → the toggle can auto-trigger today and break ~70% of surfaces). Decide: complete all ~145 dark tokens (incl. a var()-backed cockpit) vs hide the Dark/System toggle now. Quantify the cost of a full dark palette across editorial + agent + shadow + Mercury + cockpit before committing.

TRUST-AT-COMMIT TARGET: What does the IDEAL money/customer-action confirmation contain, and what data can we actually compute TODAY? The 'What this changes' / 'Money at risk' panel is the highest-stakes surface and currently ships 'preview not yet wired'. Research: which before/after/confidence/$-at-risk fields are derivable from the existing Decision/recommendation payload right now; what the minimum honest panel is (and what to omit vs fake); and how Undo SHOULD behave (countdown bound to the real undoableUntil window, branded .toast-undo styling that already exists, queue-not-evict on a second approval).

FEED FRESHNESS / OPTIMISTIC MODEL: What is the correct end-to-end optimistic-update + invalidation contract for the decision loop, and does it generalize? Define the canonical onMutate optimistic-removal + decisions.feed() invalidation pattern (the drawer path already invalidates feed; the main page doesn't), how the 'things need you' count, per-agent filter counts, and the Home Needs-You hero all stay in sync, and how a real 'alreadyHandled / stale-409' reconciliation reads ('Already handled by [teammate] · 4m ago') instead of a silent close.

AGENT EMBODIMENT: How alive should the agents be, and what is the ONE canonical avatar? Research which representation to standardize (the breathing OperatorCharacter vs the animated pixel sprites vs the serif letter-disc), how to derive its live state (busy/idle/halted → working/idle/sleep) from real agent status, and where it appears (Home verdict hero, AgentPanel header, Inbox rows, Mira desk). Validate with operators whether a subtly 'working' agent reads as trustworthy-and-present vs gimmicky/distracting — calm must not become noisy. This converts 1,100+ lines of dormant craft into the strongest aliveness signal.

IA RECONCILIATION: What is the ONE responsive nav + detail model, and where do agents live in it? Resolve: a single nav set reachable at every breakpoint (not 3 personalities); Inbox as one canonical surface with the other demoted to an explicit 'peek → view all'; a first-class 'Agents/Your team' destination where 'open agent' behaves identically everywhere (panel-sheet OR route, not both); one outcomes label (Results vs Reports vs Full reports); one halt control; and corrected help-overlay copy. Map the target route tree + breakpoint ladder before building.

ONBOARDING ACTIVATION + GATE: What is the right activation arc and the right test-gate friction? Research: should launch REQUIRE at least one passing simulated scenario (today 'Go live anyway' allows zero), and is a 'text Alex from your own phone' real-channel proof the strongest aha? Define the post-launch first-run experience that wires the orphaned useFirstRun ('Alex is live on {channel}' + a 3-step what-happens-next checklist) so the payoff lands instead of the empty 'All caught up · 0 enquiries' home. Confirm the wizard re-skin to amber CTAs + identity-hued agent mark.

MARKETING FUNNEL FLOOR + PROOF: What is the real intended first action (self-serve (auth)/onboarding vs a high-touch 'book a 15-min setup' call), and what is the minimum honest credibility anchor we can ship? Decide the CTA destination to replace mailto:, whether to lead the hero with felt pain ('Stop losing leads while you sleep') over the abstract 'revenue desk' category, and what ONE verifiable proof (a real pilot quote with clinic+city, an 'N clinics running' count, or a security line) the guard-tests will permit — so the page isn't 100% 'illustrative' to a money-on-the-line buyer.

CRAFT-SCALE BASELINES: What are the canonical scales we standardize on, sourced from the strong surfaces? Define once: a spacing scale (4/8/12/16/24/32/48/64) applied via Tailwind so --space-* stops being dead; an elevation ladder (--shadow-1..5, ONE base color, z-index-mapped) replacing the ~35 ad-hoc shadows; a single 'metric number' treatment (one face + real tabular figures, fixing the data-tabular no-op and the serif-vs-mono-vs-Inter split); 2-3 named letter-spacing tokens; and a one-page voice+lexicon guide (lowercase editorial, never blame, name the irreversible consequence, one verb per outcome, 'Later' not 'Skip' on money) — codifying what the best surfaces already do.

ACCESSIBILITY FLOOR: What is the minimum WCAG bar for launch given the audience, and what is the cost? Confirm the amber action color can darken to ~40% L (white-on-amber 3.76→4.58:1 AA) without breaking the warm feel; design the keyboard model for the Inbox card + desktop two-pane (role=button, Enter/Space, j/k/Enter/a-e triage) since the core approve flow is pointer-only today; specify the single global :focus-visible rule; and decide how a worsening ROI delta reads as gently 'cool' and legible (not alarmist red, not invisible gray) so bad news is glanceable.

SKELETON / PERCEIVED-PERFORMANCE SYSTEM: What is the shared loading-state primitive and which routes get route-level shells? Extract the agent-panel 'three-states' invariant into one <QueryStates>/useQueryGate (using the !data && !error keys-pending-safe rule that fixes the Inbox false-empty), design layout-matched skeletons for Home/Inbox/Results/Mira, add (auth) loading.tsx route shells, and decide whether to finally USE the already-installed framer-motion for AnimatePresence/FLIP list animation (killing the dead data-exiting hook + EXIT_MS race) or remove the dependency — so the daily surfaces get the polish the marketing page already has.
